evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Civil Engineer/Project Engineer
evidence.text: Interesting work, excellent coworkers, good pay and benefits, unlimited PTO for the group that I am in Good Large Company Employer

In today's competitive job market, companies that offer high pay, promote diversity and inclusion, and provide flexible work schedules are leading the way in attracting top talent. These forward-thinking policies not only foster a more inclusive and dynamic work environment but also contribute to a better work-life balance for employees. Good Work-Life Balance
Pay and benefits above average for area. Poor employee work life balance.
Good work life balance, good pay Good work life balance
Flexible work schedule and work life balance work life balance
Very flexible schedule and team. Great work-life balance
Good people, very capable firm and strong expertise with staff. Solid Experience, challenges with growth/merger

Depending on which area of the business and your team, your experience could be amazing. If not...good luck to you.. Very political and not great for black employees
Good learning opportunities with different projects A lot of learning opportunities
flexible work hours (generally), good unlimited PTO that I take advantage of, coworkers are nice Decent Company
Hybrid friendly environment. Good work culture. Fascinating work. Nice people to work with. Loan repayment program ($300 per month). Great Company Culture & Hybrid Friendly
relaxed environment and friendly teams comfortable work environment
Excellent work ethics for all Work environment
They have big engineering teams that could help you Good first job out of college
There was not any whatsoever. Poor place to work at

The absolute worst company for actually getting paid. I've been trying to get a raise for 2.5 years. I am ready to leave. Don't expect good pay

- pay; starting pay was 60k, after 6 months jumped to 72k. no bonus, project or otherwise. - travel is required and sometimes stressful; up to 5-8 sites in a day, all while driving long hours to each site. - will claim they promote upward mobility, but will black ball you if you inquire about your workload/ job position. -Lack of management and direction. You will get passed around to other managers- if you don't sp...

Senior management including the VP of Jacobs CMS (JSC) and Director of Engineering turn a blind eye to hostile work environments, they promote a bully and harassment culture, and allow project managers and mid level line managers to demean their employees. Would not recommend if looking for professional growth

evidence.location: United States Coast Guard - Air Station Sacramento
evidence.text: Long Hours: Working at Jacob might involve long hours or overtime, leading to potential burnout. High Stress: The work environment at Jacob could be fast-paced and demanding, resulting in high stress levels for employees. Limited Pay: Perhaps Jacob's salary or compensation structure isn't competitive with similar positions in the market. Lack of Recognition: Maybe Jacob doesn't have a strong system for recognizing a...

evidence.role: Current employee - Process Mechanical Engineer II
evidence.text: The company, like many corporations, is very secretive about the pay scales. The annual merit increases are small, regardless of an individual's performance rating. And the actual salary range for your position is not disclosed. Healthy Work Life Balance

evidence.role: Current employee - Junior Asset Management Consultant
evidence.text: The pay isn't nearly as good as it would be at similar companies. Job advancement isn't clear for younger employees with no clear structure for advancing. When those conversations start regarding promotion, the process is slow and there are always a million reasons for not promoting. Upskilling is difficult and it feels impossible to get any money for training. There aren't market adjustments for salaries. Great wor...
Management is not so great with distribution of work load among the team Ok
this type of work isn't for everyone Good people
Location is not the best for commuting Excellent
The pay wasn't great and the hours were long Salary Review
Lots of remote employees following Covid, felt stuck to one project - didn't learn too much Review of Civil Engineering Intern at Jacobs Engineering in Boston
not really any cons, great company overall Jacobs Solutions

evidence.role: Former employee - Quality Assurance Field Inspector
evidence.text: The liason of communication onsite was the most angry and ambiguous personality I have ever met. Imagine showing up to work on the first day, only to be told that you need to wait outside while this person "talks" with your client before you introduce yourself... Like a good ole boy office. If you are a new employee at this location and you are not spreading rumors and gossip about others, then you will fail at this...
Toxic leadership, disrespectful to employees Toxic leadership
Layoffs impacted workforce anxiety due to instability, but that's probably the same everywhere Good People, Good Leaders

evidence.role: Former employee - Instrumentation and Controls Engineer
evidence.text: Favoritism & discrimination exist in the company and do not get enough support by your mangers. Outsourcing a half of the work oversea. Bad Manager
-Understaffed at times which made the workload seem like a lot -Some office politics between the project managers and other departments Flexible/Good People

Leadership at the Tampa branch is unbelievably toxic. 3 People were hired in on our team, showed up to work for 1 week and quit the next. They have no experience working in a professional organization. Don't expect to be treated like a human being. TOXIC
